title: System for investigating biological structures
abstract: A system 1 for investigating biological structures, the system 1 comprising: a probe 9 for application to a subject under investigation, said probe comprising a plurality of electrodes 11 arranged in an array, the electrodes of said array being operable to apply electrical signals to said subject and to receive signals that are indicative of electrical properties of said subject; a probe controller 5 responsive to control signals from a controller to select electrodes of said plurality to form a sensor, said sensor being operable to apply electrical signals to a region of said subject and to output received signals that are indicative of electrical properties of said region of said subject; a measurement module 7 coupled to the probe controller for the receipt of said received signals, said measurement module being operable to determine from said received signals the electrical properties of said region of the subject; and a system controller 3 operable to output control signals to said probe controller, the control signals specifying the electrodes of the array that are to be selected to form said sensor
claims: Claims 1. A system for investigating biological structures, the system comprising: a probe for application to a subject under investigation, said probe comprising a plurality of electrodes arranged in an array, the electrodes of said array being operable to apply electrical signals to said subject and to receive signals that are indicative of electrical properties of said subject; a probe controller responsive to control signals from a controller to select electrodes of said plurality to form a sensor, said sensor being operable to apply electrical signals to a region of said subject and to output received signals that are indicative of electrical properties of said region of said subject; a measurement module coupled to the probe controller for the receipt of said received signals, said measurement module being operable to determine from said received signals the electrical properties of said region of the subject; and a system controller operable to output control signals to said probe controller, the control signals specifying the electrodes of the array that are to be selected to form said sensor. 2. A system according to Claim 1 , wherein said sensor comprises first and second electrodes for the application of an electrical signal to said region of the subject. 3. A system according to Claim 2, wherein said sensor comprises third and fourth electrodes for the receipt of signals that are indicative of an electrical property of the region of the subject. 4. A system according to Claim 2 and 3, wherein said sensor comprises a tetrapole. 5. A system according to Claim 4, wherein electrodes of said tetrapole are linearly arranged. 6. A system according to Claim 5, wherein the linearly arranged electrodes of said tetrapole comprise a line of four electrodes that are immediately adjacent to one another. 7. A system according to Claim 5, wherein the linearly arranged electrodes of said tetrapole comprise a line of four electrodes that are each spaced from one another by one or more electrodes. 8. A system according to any of Claims 1 to 7, wherein said system controller is operable to output a plurality of control signals each specifying a different selection of sensors for investigating different regions of said subject, the arrangement being such that the control signals define a sensor that tracks across the subject to investigate multiple regions of the subject. 9. A system according to Claim 8, wherein the control signals each specify a different selection of sensors for investigating different linearly arranged regions of said subject. 10. A system according to Claim 9, wherein the control signals are configured to track the sensor over the subject in different directions to investigate multiple linearly arranged regions of said subject. 11. A system according to Claim 4, wherein the electrodes of said tetrapole are arranged in a polygon, such as a square or rectangle. 12. A system according to Claim 11 , wherein said system controller is operable to output a plurality of control signals each specifying a different polygonally arranged selection of sensors for investigating different regions of said subject, the arrangement being such that the control signals define a sensor that tracks across the subject in concentric polygons to investigate multiple regions of the subject. 13. A system according to any preceding claim, wherein said received signals are indicative of the electrical impedance of the region of the subject investigated by the sensor. 14. A system according to any preceding claim, wherein said sensor is operable to apply electrical signals at multiple frequencies to said region of said subject. 15. A system according to Claim 14, wherein said measurement module is operable to determine tissue type from received signals.
